message:
fix(e2e): fix UPDATE-by-id normalizer whitespace handling before WHERE clause

The normalize_update_by_id parser required whitespace immediately
before WHERE via consume_required_ascii_whitespace, but after the
last assignment value, whitespace was consumed by the trailing
consume_ascii_whitespace in the loop. This caused the WHERE-check
to fail when the whitespace had already been consumed.

Fix: track whether whitespace was consumed after the last assignment
via has_where_separator flag, and check that instead of requiring
additional whitespace before WHERE. This correctly handles cases
like "SET status = 'active', created_at = 3600 WHERE id = 7"
while rejecting "SET status = 'active'WHERE id = 7" (no space).

New tests:
- normalize_update_by_id_extracts_one_prepared_shape_and_params:
  verifies correct parameter extraction for multi-assignment UPDATE
- normalize_update_by_id_rejects_missing_where_separator: ensures
  missing space before WHERE is rejected

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
